content:
🔥 Example from Whiplash
• Andrew is constantly forced into escalating, high-pressure decisions:
• Does he keep drumming after being humiliated? (Yes → He pushes harder.)
• Does he quit after Fletcher sabotages him? (No → He fights back onstage.)
• The film builds momentum because each choice leads to a point of no return.

McCloud: Stories Progress Through Space and Reader Control

McCloud flips this idea—in comics, time isn’t dictated by narrative alone but also by how the reader moves through the page.

“The panel acts as a unit of time. The spacing between them controls pacing.”

🔥 Example from Watchmen
• Dr. Manhattan’s flashback sequences use a strict nine-panel grid to make time feel mechanical, reinforcing his perception of reality.
• Unlike film, where the director controls timing, the reader decides how long they spend on each panel.

📌 Key Takeaway for Graphic Novels:
context: From document: 1.1.3. | Narrative Transcends Medium | Subheading — chunk 2 (chunk 2/7)
